title: genista acanthoclada subsp. graeca
intro: Genista acanthoclada subsp. graeca, also known as Greek Spiny Broom or Graecan Spiny Broom, is a plant species in the pea family (Fabaceae) that is native to the Balkan Peninsula. Its medicinal properties are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of this plant include the treatment of various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin problems, as well as its use as a diuretic and astringent. In traditional herbal medicine, Genista acanthoclada subsp. graeca is prepared as an infusion, tincture, or decoction, and is often used to support digestive health and immune function.
